title: Memory for Autonomous LLM Agents: Mechanisms, Evaluation, and Emerging Frontiers
storage_format: NA — survey that proposes a three-dimensional taxonomy (temporal scope, representational substrate, control policy) and classifies 5 families of mechanisms: in-context compression, retrieval stores, reflective memory, hierarchical/virtual memory, and policy-learned memory
agent_framework: NA — survey covers RAG, Reflexion, Generative Agents, Voyager, MemGPT, MemoryBank, ExpeL, ChatDB, AgeMem, among others
benchmarks_used: NA — analyzes LoCoMo, MemBench, MemoryAgentBench, MemoryArena as memory evaluation benchmarks
performance_metrics: NA — proposes a 4-layer stack: task effectiveness, memory quality, efficiency, governance
main_result: Survey that formalizes agent memory as a write-manage-read loop coupled to the POMDP cycle. Proposes a taxonomy with 3 dimensions (temporal scope, representational substrate, control policy) and identifies consolidation, causal retrieval, reliable reflection, and learned forgetting as the most critical open challenges.
limitations: No formal search/selection methodology for the survey; no original empirical data; coverage may be incomplete; comparative analysis based on qualitative descriptions of the systems
validity_threats: Article selection without a reproducible protocol; proposed taxonomy not empirically validated; conclusions about gaps based on qualitative observation of the field; single-author survey without formal peer review (preprint)
